title: Pentatherum ruprechtii
intro: Pentatherum ruprechtii, also known as Tansy, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Pentatherum ruprechtii include sesquiterpene lactones, such as thiarubrine A, and essential oils, including camphor and borneol. Traditionally, herbal preparations made from Pentatherum ruprechtii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat digestive problems and promote skin health.
taxonomy: Pentatherum ruprechtii is classified as a species within the plant family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pentatherum. The plant's classification at the kingdom level is Plantae. At the phylum level, it is Angiospermophyta. This classification places Pentatherum ruprechtii within the broader taxonomic group of flowering plants.

It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation.
